blocks.text: NESCO Renewable Energy Overview
Northern Electricity Supply PLC — Contributing to Bangladesh's Clean Energy Transition

NESCO is steadily expanding its renewable energy footprint across northern Bangladesh through rooftop solar, floating solar, ground-mounted plants, and partnerships with independent power producers. This overview presents NESCO's current renewable energy portfolio and upcoming plans to scale clean generation across its service area.
Snapshot at a Glance
1. Rooftop Solar with Net Metering
NESCO has facilitated the installation of rooftop solar systems for customers across its territory, with eligible systems connected under the national net metering framework.
Total rooftop solar systems: 297 — capacity 11.726 MWp
Of which under net metering: 144 systems — capacity 10.6 MWp
2. Floating Solar System
1 floating solar installation — capacity 750 kWp
Customer: Nawab Auto Rice Mill, Chapai-Nawabganj
3. NESCO's Own Rooftop Solar (Office & Substation Buildings)
NESCO has installed rooftop solar systems on its own office buildings and substation control rooms to power its operations with clean energy.
Total installations: 40 systems — combined capacity 400 kW
Operational status: 32 systems active; 8 systems currently offline
4. Self-Financed OPEX-Model Rooftop Solar
Under NESCO's own OPEX model, rooftop solar has been deployed at five commercial and industrial customer sites:
Total: 5 systems — combined capacity 60 kW

rows: Mallik Paper Mills, Civil Haat, Paksey, Ishwardi
BCL Cold Storage, Thengamara, Karatoa, Bogura
Uttara Seed Cold Storage, Notkhana, Nilphamari
Nuha Auto Rice Mills, Kukhapara, Nilphamari

blocks.text: 5. Solar Battery Charging Station
1 station — capacity 25 kW
Location: Rajshahi
Funded by: GIZ
6. Direct Power Evacuation from Solar IPPs
In addition to NESCO's own systems, the following independent solar power plants directly evacuate power into NESCO's 33/11 kV substations:
Intraco Solar Plant — 30 MW — Kaliganj, Lalmonirhat
Sympa Solar Plant — 8 MW — Tetulia
Northwest Power Generation Company Solar Plant — 7.5 MW — Sirajganj
Future Plans for Renewable Expansion
Ground-Mounted Solar Opportunities
Nurpur, Pabna: NESCO owns approximately 5 acres suitable for a ground-mounted solar system of around 2 MWp capacity.
Additional NESCO-owned land available for ground-mounted solar at Kaunia (Rangpur), Sirajganj, and Pachbibi (Joypurhat).
National Rooftop Solar Programme — Initiative-B, Model-3 (Third-Party OPEX)
NESCO floated a tender under the national rooftop solar programme covering 1,903 educational and health institutions, for a combined rooftop solar capacity of 72.4 MW across 4 packages and 10 lots.
Tender opening date: 26 February 2026

rows: Design, Supply, Installation, Testing & Commissioning of Rooftop Solar System

blocks.text: Status update: The tenders were issued in accordance with the Power Division's prescribed tender documents, with submission deadlines extended from time to time per Power Division instructions. As no bids were received, the tenders were closed on 26 February 2026. NESCO is reviewing next steps for re-tendering this programme.
Looking Ahead

NESCO remains committed to scaling renewable energy across northern Bangladesh through a combination of utility-owned solar assets, customer-side rooftop programmes, and partnerships with private solar developers. With available land for ground-mounted projects and a pipeline of rooftop tenders under preparation, NESCO is positioned to make a meaningful contribution to the national clean energy targets.
